name: Vibe Design System
what_it_does: Vibe is monday.com's official open-source React UI component library and design system, providing pre-built components, an icon set, testing utilities, and an MCP server that gives AI agents intelligent guidance on component APIs, usage examples, and icon selection.
use_cases: Building monday.com apps or internal tools that need to visually match monday.com's design language
Using an AI coding assistant with the @vibe/mcp server to get accurate Vibe component API guidance and examples without hallucination
Rapidly prototyping React UIs with a comprehensive, accessible, and well-documented component library
not_for: Teams building products that should not look like monday.com — the opinionated design language is very specific
Projects needing a component library without a third-party brand dependency
best_when: You are building a monday.com app or integration where visual consistency with the monday.com platform is required, and you want AI-assisted development with accurate component guidance.
avoid_when: Your project needs a design-system-agnostic or brand-neutral component library.

auth.notes: No authentication required for the component library or MCP server.

pricing.notes: Open source. No licensing costs for the library itself.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: MCP server is a documentation/guidance tool rather than an action executor, limiting error surface.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: MCP server provides read-only component API guidance — fully idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: MCP server assists with component usage but agents still need React knowledge to generate correct code
Version 3 has breaking changes from Vibe 2 — ensure agents use v3 docs
monday.com-specific design tokens may conflict with project's existing CSS
Monorepo structure means installing @vibe/core, @vibe/icons, etc. separately
